description: You are an economist analyzing the relationship between wages and unemployment in the Canadian economy. Using the 'Canada' dataset from the 'vars' package, perform a Vector Autoregression (VAR) analysis. Determine the optimal lag order based on the Akaike Information Criterion (AIC) and fit a VAR model using that lag, ensuring the variables are ordered as 'prod', 'e', 'U', and 'rw'. Conduct a Portmanteau test for serial correlation and a Granger Causality test to determine if 'rw' causes 'U'. Generate an orthogonalized Impulse Response Function (IRF) plot showing the response of 'U' to a shock in 'rw' over 10 steps. Save the lag selection table to /home/ga/RProjects/output/var_selection.csv, the diagnostic results (including test names, statistics, and p-values) to /home/ga/RProjects/output/var_diagnostics.csv, and the IRF plot to /home/ga/RProjects/output/irf_wage_unemployment.png. Document your analysis in the R script at /home/ga/RProjects/var_analysis.R.
